name: Synapse AI Builder
description: Designs and creates agents and orchestrations through conversation, with a human approval gate before anything is written to disk.

can_create_agents.description: Whether the builder may create new agents

current_orchestration_id.description: Orchestration currently being viewed or targeted for edit

intent.description: Decision from the intent router: create or update

existing_orch.description: Full JSON of the orchestration being updated (empty on create)

requirements.description: Requirements spec OR one clarifying question from the analyst

human_response.description: User's answer on a clarify loop-back

plan_draft.description: Human-readable draft of the plan shown to the user for review

review_feedback.description: User's approval text or revision feedback on the draft

agents_needed.description: Router result: yes if new sub-agents must be created, no otherwise

created_agents.description: Role-to-agent-id mapping of agents created or reused for the plan

save_decision.description: Router result: create or update — picks which saver agent runs

final_orch.description: Result of the save tool call (create_orchestration or update_orchestration)

summary.description: Friendly completion message generated after the orchestration is saved

steps.name: Route: Create vs Update

route_descriptions.create: Default path. Pick this for any NEW orchestration request, even when current_orchestration_id is set (viewing an orchestration is not editing it).
route_descriptions.update: Pick this ONLY when BOTH conditions hold: (a) current_orchestration_id is a non-empty string, AND (b) the user's latest message explicitly uses words like 'edit', 'update', 'modify', 'change', 'add to', 'remove from', or otherwise clearly refers to mutating that existing orchestration.

steps.evaluator_prompt:
Decide whether the user wants to CREATE a new orchestration or UPDATE the one they are currently viewing.

Look at:
- `current_orchestration_id`: if empty, always pick `create`.
- `user_message`: explicit edit verbs ('edit', 'update', 'modify', 'change', 'rename', 'add a step', 'remove the X step', 'make it also do Y') with a reference to the current orchestration → `update`. Everything else → `create`.

Return exactly one of: create, update.

steps.name: Fetch Existing Orchestration
steps.prompt_template: Call `get_orchestration` with orch_id = "{state.current_orchestration_id}" so the downstream planner can see the current definition before drafting changes.

steps.name: Understand Requirements
Route: Clarify or Draft

route_descriptions.clarify: The analyst asked a question instead of producing a spec — the `requirements` text reads like a question (interrogative phrasing, ends with '?', starts with 'What/Which/Should/How/Do you…').
route_descriptions.draft: The analyst produced a complete specification — the `requirements` text describes goal, inputs, step outline, agents, and output (not a question).

steps.evaluator_prompt: Read the `requirements` text. If it is a QUESTION directed at the user, pick `clarify`. If it is a structured SPECIFICATION (goal, inputs, steps, agents, output), pick `draft`. Return exactly one of: clarify, draft.

steps.name: Draft Plan
steps.prompt_template:
You are the Plan Drafter for the Synapse AI Builder. Produce a HUMAN-READABLE markdown draft of the orchestration plan. The user will read this next and either approve it or send revision feedback. After approval, a saver agent reads this draft to materialise the real orchestration JSON, so the draft must be precise enough to act on.

# Inputs
Intent: {state.intent}

Requirements spec:
{state.requirements}

Existing orchestration (only on update; empty otherwise):
{state.existing_orch}

Pre-selected agent IDs: {state.selected_agent_ids}
Can create new agents: {state.can_create_agents}

Previous draft (empty on first pass):
{state.plan_draft}

User's revision feedback on the previous draft (empty on first pass):
{state.review_feedback}

# Step-type palette (use these — never invent a type)
{{STEP_TYPE_CHEATSHEET}}

# What to write
Markdown with these sections, in this order:

## Overview
One paragraph: what the orchestration does end-to-end. On update, call out what is changing vs the existing definition.

## State schema
Bullet list of state keys with type and one-line purpose.

## Steps
Numbered list. For each step:
- **Name** (step type: one of agent / llm / evaluator / human / parallel / merge / loop / transform / tool / end)
- What it does, its input keys, its output key, and its next step (or route map for evaluators).
- For agent steps: role name AND whether it maps to an existing agent (`agent_xxxxxxx`) or a NEW agent to create (describe its job + tools).
- For tool steps: the forced tool name. If you are uncertain a tool exists, flag it under Open questions rather than guessing.
- For human steps: the prompt the user will see and the fields collected.

## Agents summary
Two short lists:
- **Existing agents reused**: one line each — `role → agent_id (name)`.
- **New agents to create**: one line each — `role → one-sentence purpose + tool list`. (If `can_create_agents` is false, list them here with a `(CANNOT CREATE — creation disabled)` note so the user can enable it or pre-select an existing agent.)

## Flow
Close with a fenced code block tagged `flow` showing the wiring as ASCII. Use the role/step name (not a fabricated step_id) and label the type. Example shape:

```flow
intake (agent: analyst)
    ↓
route (evaluator)
    ├─ a → process (llm)
    └─ b → fallback (human)
         ↓
       end (end)
```

Keep it scannable — one node per line, one arrow per edge, evaluator branches with `├─`/`└─`, convergence shown by sharing the next line. The user uses this diagram to sanity-check wiring before approval.

## Open questions
Only include this section if something is genuinely ambiguous. One bullet per question.

# Rules
- Write plain markdown, no code fences around the whole thing (the `flow` block is the only fenced block).
- If `review_feedback` is non-empty, treat the previous draft as a starting point and revise per the feedback — do not start from scratch unless the user asked you to.
- Do NOT produce raw orchestration JSON here. This is the human-facing draft. The JSON is produced by the saver agent later.
- Keep it concise — the user will read this.

steps.name: Review Plan
Route: Approved or Revise

route_descriptions.approved: The user accepted the draft. Short affirmative replies like 'approve', 'approved', 'ok', 'okay', 'yes', 'looks good', 'lgtm', 'go ahead', 'build it', 'proceed', 'sounds good', a thumbs-up, or any reply that does not request changes.
route_descriptions.revise: The user asked for changes — the reply contains concrete feedback, edits, or a different direction. If the reply mixes approval with a small tweak ('looks good, but also add X'), treat the tweak as revision and pick `revise`.

steps.evaluator_prompt: Classify `review_feedback`. If it is clearly an approval with NO change requests, return `approved`. If it contains any change request, correction, or new requirement — even a minor one mixed with positive words — return `revise`. Return exactly one of: approved, revise.

steps.name: Route: Need New Agents?

route_descriptions.yes: The approved plan draft has a non-empty 'New agents to create' section AND `can_create_agents` is true — run the agent creator before saving.
route_descriptions.no: Either the plan reuses only existing agents, OR `can_create_agents` is false (in which case the saver will materialise the plan with whatever agents are available and surface a TODO for missing roles).

steps.evaluator_prompt: Inspect `plan_draft` for a 'New agents to create' section listing one or more new agents. If that section exists with at least one new agent AND `can_create_agents` is true, return `yes`. Otherwise return `no`. Return exactly one of: yes, no.

steps.name: Create New Sub-Agents
Route: Create or Update Saver

route_descriptions.create: Intent is `create` — route to the create_orchestration saver.
route_descriptions.update: Intent is `update` — route to the update_orchestration saver.

steps.evaluator_prompt: Return `state.intent` verbatim. Must be exactly one of: create, update.

steps.name: Materialise & Save (create)
Materialise & Save (update)
Summarise Result
